channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
item.description: AI Engineer: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents lack sufficient context and memory of prior interactions, limiting their effectiveness despite individual capability. He uses the analogy of a talented engineer with amnesia to illustrate the problem and frames this as a core challenge needing resolution in agent development.

item.title: Fable 5 Returns... This Is Big
item.description: AI Revolution: The video claims Fable 5 may be returning after being pulled for national security reasons. It cites leaked Claude code strings, Amazon Bedrock confirmations, Congressional pressure, and a reported Mythos successor, suggesting government scrutiny and policy negotiations are reshaping availability.

item.title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
item.description: AI Engineer: Ishaan Sehgal argues that agent identity and continuity should be centered on durable session logs rather than models, tools, or orchestration frameworks. He draws an analogy to save files in video games to illustrate that the log, not the compute or framework, is the persistent entity that defines an agent across different runtimes and machines.

item.title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
item.description: AI Engineer: Raymond Weitekamp examines Recursive Language Models and how their inference-time compute patterns can be applied to coding agents. The approach aims to improve performance and reliability in agent behavior.

item.title: When Forgiveness Was a Trap - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Ada Palmer on the topic of forgiveness in historical context. The discussion explores how forgiveness functioned as a social mechanism, potentially as a constraint or tool of control in past societies.

item.title: GTA VI + GPT-6: Rockstar's AI Mechanics Revealed
item.description: AI Master: A YouTube video from AI Master claims that GTA VI does not use GPT-6 and instead relies on Rockstar's proprietary AI stack based on RAGE 9 engine with custom patents for witness systems and NPC memory. The creator argues that viral "GTA VI + GPT-6" clips are fabricated and discusses the actual AI technologies Rockstar has deployed for real-time behavioral simulation in gaming.

item.title: What's new with data agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented new tools for building data analytics agents, including the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations. These tools are accessible via Gemini CLI, Claude Code, Codex, and IDEs like VSCode to streamline workflows in data engineering, data science, and conversational analytics.

item.title: Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
item.description: AI Engineer: Nishant Gupta from Meta Superintelligence Labs argues that traditional offline benchmarks and static datasets fail for autonomous AI systems in production. He presents architectural patterns for building continuous evaluation pipelines that measure tool use, planning, reasoning, drift detection, and operational reliability rather than model accuracy alone.

item.title: Build Systems, Not Code - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation
item.description: AI Engineer: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents shift engineering focus from writing individual lines of code to designing agentic systems at a higher level of abstraction. The core engineering disciplines, systems thinking, decomposition, separation of concerns, and state management, remain essential, with engineering value moving to the system architecture layer.

item.title: Anthropic's Labs Lead On Fable's Capabilities + Building AI-Native Products — With Mike Krieger
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Mike Krieger, head of Anthropic Labs, discusses Fable and Mythos in the context of government-forced model removals. He describes how AI-native product development accelerates workflows and explains Anthropic's safety posture as material rather than marketing-driven.

item.title: Power intelligent agents with AI-native databases
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: AI-native databases enable intelligent agents with enterprise data through AI-powered search, natural language interfaces, and multimodal queries. The session covers graph retrieval-augmented generation and open standards like Model Context Protocol to provide interconnected context for agents.

item.title: Boost AI context with hybrid search in Spanner
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented hybrid search capabilities in Spanner that combine full-text and vector search for AI-powered applications. The feature supports retrieval-augmented generation and integrates with Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform for contextual multi-model search.

item.title: Agent development and AgentOps with BigQuery, ADK, and MCP
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud's Agent Development Kit (ADK) is a framework for building agents that integrate with data sources like BigQuery, Google Maps, and AlloyDB using standardized connectors. The ADK reduces the need for custom database connector development by implementing Model Context Protocol (MCP) for tool connections.

item.title: Build connected AI: Orchestrate tools and agents with registries and ADK
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech presented guidance on building production AI agents using the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Agent Registry. The session covered integrating agents with existing systems via Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers and building custom MCP servers to expose APIs as agent-ready tools.

item.title: What's new with Gemini from Google DeepMind
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google DeepMind presented updates to Gemini including upgraded model reasoning, expanded context window processing, and enhanced multimodal tool use. The presentation showcased workflows for building agentic applications with Gemini on Vertex AI and discussed research advancements in agent design.

item.title: Building an AI app: A low-code guide for small teams
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud's low-code guide enables small teams to build AI applications without extensive AI expertise by leveraging corporate data, Cloud SQL, AlloyDB for PostgreSQL, and Gemini for natural language interfaces, hybrid search, and recommendations. The approach auto-generates code to rapidly deploy intelligent features in minutes.

item.title: What's new in Looker: Empowering business users in the governed agentic era
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented new Looker capabilities including dashboard agents that automate workflows, conversational analytics, and semantic model enhancements integrated with Gemini. The platform emphasizes grounding AI in a governed semantic layer to enable business users in an agentic era.

item.title: NoSQL for modern apps and AI: The future of Memorystore, Firestore, and Bigtable
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented advancements in NoSQL databases across Memorystore, Firestore, and Bigtable. The session covered product releases, architectural innovations, and customer stories designed to support AI and modern application workloads.

item.title: Implementing DeepMind innovation: Deep research API
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google DeepMind has developed a Deep Research API that uses Gemini-powered agents to help complete comprehensive research tasks. The API enables developers to implement these agentic techniques in their applications through Google Cloud.

item.title: What's new in Cloud Run
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced new capabilities in Cloud Run for deploying AI agent and inference workloads with improved serverless scaling and operational efficiency. The session covers latest features enabling seamless application scaling while reducing operational overhead for AI-driven development.

item.title: What's new in Cloud SQL: Drive performance, high availability, and security
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud SQL presentation covering new features for MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server including AI-driven automated health management, fleet-wide insights, and Database Migration Service for cloud transitions. Focus is on performance, high availability, and security improvements.

item.title: What's new in Google Cloud's agent platform
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced its updated agent platform featuring a unified Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform with Agent Builder for no-code/low-code agent creation, an Agent Registry and marketplace for distributing reusable components, and enterprise governance tools including observability, evaluation, and simulation capabilities. The platform includes an expanded Agent Development Kit with language support and native agent-to-agent communication.

item.title: The Miranda Hypothesis: How Hamilton Poisoned Persona Evals - Jacob E. Thomas, Results Gen
item.description: AI Engineer: Jacob E. Thomas argues that current persona evaluation benchmarks fail to detect "Miranda distortion," where cultural representations in training data overwhelm primary documentary records, causing persona systems to replicate cultural composites rather than specified characters. He proposes a four-stage paradigm shift to epistemic simulation with domain expert evaluation loops and introduces the pre-registered Prism Experiment using historical figures and anachronism detection rubrics.

item.title: What's new for AI on GKE: Training, serving, and agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Kubernetes Engine has added new capabilities for AI model training, serving, and reinforcement learning workloads. The platform supports agentic workflows and is optimized for reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness. Companies like Thinking Machines Lab and Baseten use GKE to run their AI infrastructure.

item.title: Engineering the future of Kubernetes for AI at scale
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud proposes that Kubernetes infrastructure must evolve to support AI agents, inference workloads, and batch processing with hardware-aware elasticity. The session covers how Google's contributions to Kubernetes are enabling infrastructure fluidity, agentic workflow security, and resource optimization for AI-scale applications.

item.title: Unsure how to approach CS50? Carter Zenke answers.
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's introduction to computer science and programming, available through edX, Harvard Extension School, and OpenCourseWare. The course is taught by David J. Malan and maintains active communities across Discord, GitHub, Reddit, and other platforms.

item.title: Kimi K2.7 Code: THIS is VERY CRAZY MAN!
item.description: AICodeKing: Moonshot AI released Kimi K2.7 Code, an open-source agentic coding model with a Mixture-of-Experts architecture, one trillion parameters, and 256K context window. The accompanying Kimi Code CLI enables terminal-based repository exploration, file editing, command execution, and testing with plan mode for investigating codebases before making changes.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 3 Livestream
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 held its final day of main-stage programming on July 2, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco. The livestream featured keynotes, talks, and a startup battlefield competition, serving as a major industry gathering point.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 2 Livestream
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 is a conference held at Moscone West in San Francisco on July 1, 2026. The event features keynote sessions and main-stage programming throughout the day, livestreamed online.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 1: Software Factories & Keynotes
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 1 livestream from San Francisco featuring keynote sessions and main-stage programming on software factories and AI engineering. The event runs June 30, 2026 at Moscone West with sessions scheduled throughout the day.

item.title: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities - BBC
item.description: Google News AI: Anthropic has accused Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities, according to a BBC report. The accusation suggests unauthorized access to or replication of AI model functionality.

item.title: Ask HN: Where is our profession (programmer) going?
item.description: syntaxbush: A programmer observes that at a visited software company, LLMs like Claude have become the primary tool for code generation and problem-solving, with human code review, abstraction practices, and deep problem understanding being de-emphasized. The author expresses concern that this represents a shift from precise, knowledge-intensive work toward probabilistic, outsourced decision-making.

item.title: The Agentic OS Setup That Will 10x Claude Code
item.description: Chase AI: Chase AI argues that building an Agentic OS architecture improves Claude Code outputs through shared fundamentals like loop engineering, memory management, and skills design. The setup offers customization, visual interfaces, and enables knowledge sharing with non-technical teams.

item.title: [AINews] It's Meta-Harness Summer
item.description: Latent Space: Latent Space discusses a shift toward Meta's harness approaches for evaluating AI systems, suggesting Meta-based testing harnesses are becoming the dominant standard in the industry. The piece presents this as a significant trend shift in AI evaluation methodology.

item.title: Bible as RAG Database
item.description: jacksonastone: A discussion or exploration of using the Bible as a retrieval-augmented generation database. This applies RAG techniques to religious text retrieval and reference systems.

item.title: What I'm Finding About LLM Code Style and Token Costs
item.description: jimmont: The author reports observations about how different code styles affect token consumption when using LLMs. This is a practical analysis of code formatting patterns and their impact on inference cost, based on experimental findings.

item.title: Europe is pushing back on Washington’s chip war
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Europe is resisting U.S. export restrictions on semiconductor manufacturing equipment as part of Washington's broader strategy to contain Chinese chip capabilities. ASML CEO notes that current Chinese access is limited to older deep ultraviolet lithography tools from a decade ago, which the MATCH Act would further restrict.

item.title: We'll fight the platform war against Big AI
item.description: aendruk: An author signals intent to challenge the dominance of large AI platforms and incumbent companies. The framing suggests a platform competition dynamic between centralized AI incumbents and alternative approaches.

item.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
item.description: Cole Medin: Cole Medin discusses Google's 50-page playbook on agentic engineering, arguing that the AI model accounts for only 10% of coding outcomes while the surrounding harness of context, tools, guardrails, and verification comprises the other 90%. The video covers the spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering, the importance of context engineering, and the evolution of developer roles from conductor to orchestrator.

item.title: simonw/browser-compat-db
item.description: Simon Willison: Simon Willison created simonw/browser-compat-db, a SQLite database conversion of Mozilla's browser compatibility data. The project includes Claude-generated Python scripts for data transformation, a GitHub Actions workflow built with GPT-4.5, and makes the database publicly accessible via GitHub CDN with CORS support for exploration via Datasette Lite.

item.title: What we learned from 1,604 Chinese AI job postings
item.description: Epoch AI: Epoch AI analyzed 1,604 Chinese AI job postings to infer the research strategies and priorities of Chinese AI labs. The analysis uses hiring patterns as a window into organizational focus areas and technology direction.

item.title: Former Infosys chief has a new startup that wants to challenge the IT services world
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Former Infosys CEO Vishal Sikka has launched a new startup backed by Mayfield and Aramco Ventures. The company brings together veterans from SAP, Infosys, and VianAI to challenge the traditional IT services sector.

item.title: Anthropic Just Replaced Claude Code With New Claude Tag
item.description: AI Revolution: Anthropic launched Claude Tag, a team-focused version of Claude Code that integrates natively with Slack, connects to company tools, and replaces the previous Claude Slack chatbot. The tool is in research preview with plans for wider rollout.

item.title: Omnigent: Open-source meta harness for agents
item.description: chatmasta: Omnigent is an open-source framework that provides a meta harness for agents. It appears designed to standardize or simplify the development and deployment of AI agent systems.

item.title: Cerebras stock plunges after earnings as CEO says margin outlook was misunderstood
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Cerebras, an AI chipmaker, reported lower-than-expected gross margins in its first earnings as a public company, causing its stock price to decline. The CEO attributed the market reaction to a misunderstanding of the company's margin outlook.

item.title: A24 Knows You're Mad About the Google AI Collab
item.description: nate: A24 is facing backlash from its audience over a collaboration with Google AI. The announcement has generated negative public sentiment regarding the entertainment company's partnership with the tech giant.

item.title: AI was supposed to kill engineering jobs, but new data suggests they’re the most resilient
item.description: TechCrunch AI: TechCrunch reports on SignalFire data showing that engineering jobs are resilient despite predictions of AI-driven displacement. Engineers represent a larger share of new hires in AI-affected sectors, contradicting narratives about widespread job elimination.

item.title: AI Data Center Power Requirements in 2026: The Complete Grid-to-Chip Guide
item.description: lopespm: lopespm presents an analysis of projected AI data center power consumption through 2026, covering infrastructure scaling from grid to chip-level systems. The piece examines power requirements as AI workloads expand.

item.title: AI researchers continue to leave Google for its rivals
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Top AI researchers Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel are departing Google to join Anthropic, continuing a trend of high-profile scientist exits from Google that previously included Noam Shazeer and John Jumper. This reflects competitive pressure in hiring and retaining AI talent.

item.title: OpenAI Codex bombards SSDs with needless write operations
item.description: jgalt212: OpenAI Codex generates excessive write operations to SSDs, potentially damaging storage hardware through unnecessary disk activity. The report highlights inefficient I/O behavior in the model's execution pipeline.
